lastReleaseMessage: Course-refresh cron moved off GitHub Actions onto local Task Scheduler.

barRaise.topFinding: The release pipeline has seized. For a fourth straight bar-raise the same four HIGH findings stand unremediated, and since the 2026-05-29 review not one file in the release-blocking set has changed: the working tree still holds the full 0.33.1 + 0.34.0 cut (real code in worker/src/index.js, src/profile.ts, tools/force_refresh_courses.py) uncommitted for five days, the last version commit is still 2d0ef0f v0.33.0 from 2026-05-25, and the only commit since the last review (896fcd4, .project-context.json) is orthogonal scaffolding. The fix that unblocks the ship is small and known: scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1 still hardcodes audit.findings to 0/0/0 (line 98) and nulls the entire barRaise block (lines 103-110), so running release.ps1 before that fix lands erases this report and zeroes the dashboard. The path is unchanged from three days ago: make the dashboard writer merge-aware, then run release.ps1. The verdict is stalled not because the project is dead (the course-refresh cron drained on schedule at 04:00 today and bar-raises run daily) but because the at-risk remediation has not moved at all.
